channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: WorkRB: A Community-Driven Evaluation Framework for AI in the Work Domain
item.description: arXiv:2604.13055v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Today's evolving labor markets rely increasingly on recommender systems for hiring, talent management, and workforce analytics, with natural language processing (NLP) capabilities at the core. Yet, research in this area remains highly fragmented. Stud...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, research, study, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Text-as-Signal: Quantitative Semantic Scoring with Embeddings, Logprobs, and Noise Reduction
item.description: arXiv:2604.13056v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents a practical pipeline for turning text corpora into quantitative semantic signals. Each news item is represented as a full-document embedding, scored through logprob-based evaluation over a configurable positional dictionary, and pr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, ICL, study, embedding, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: KMMMU: Evaluation of Massive Multi-discipline Multimodal Understanding in Korean Language and Context
item.description: arXiv:2604.13058v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce KMMMU, a native Korean benchmark for evaluating multimodal understanding in Korean cultural and institutional settings. KMMMU contains 3,466 questions from exams natively written in Korean, covering nine disciplines and nine visual modali...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, model, few-shot, experiment, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mathematical Reasoning Enhanced LLM for Formula Derivation: A Case Study on Fiber NLI Modellin
item.description: arXiv:2604.13062v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong capabilities in code generation and text synthesis, yet their potential for symbolic physical reasoning in domain-specific scientific problems remains underexplored. We present a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, large language model, study, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GoodPoint: Learning Constructive Scientific Paper Feedback from Author Responses
item.description: arXiv:2604.11924v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While LLMs hold significant potential to transform scientific research, we advocate for their use to augment and empower researchers rather than to automate research without human oversight. To this end, we study constructive feedback generation, the ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, research, fine-tuning, ICL | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Long-Horizon Task Mirage? Diagnosing Where and Why Agentic Systems Break
item.description: arXiv:2604.11978v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents perform strongly on short- and mid-horizon tasks, but often break down on long-horizon tasks that require extended, interdependent action sequences. Despite rapid progress in agentic systems, these long-horizon failur...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, release, API, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: When to Forget: A Memory Governance Primitive
item.description: arXiv:2604.12007v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent memory systems accumulate experience but currently lack a principled operational metric for memory quality governance -- deciding which memories to trust, suppress, or deprecate as the agent's task distribution shifts. Write-time importance scor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, experiment, memory, embedding, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Identity as Attractor: Geometric Evidence for Persistent Agent Architecture in LLM Activation Space
item.description: arXiv:2604.12016v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models map semantically related prompts to similar internal representations -- a phenomenon interpretable as attractor-like dynamics. We ask whether the identity document of a persistent cognitive agent (its cognitive_core) exhibits ana...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, large language model, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A longitudinal health agent framework
item.description: arXiv:2604.12019v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although artificial intelligence (AI) agents are increasingly proposed to support potentially longitudinal health tasks, such as symptom management, behavior change, and patient support, most current implementations fall short of facilitating user int...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, alignment, paper, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Design Conditions for Intra-Group Learning of Sequence-Level Rewards: Token Gradient Cancellation
item.description: arXiv:2604.13088v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In sparse termination rewards, intra-group comparisons have become the dominant paradigm for fine-tuning reasoning models via reinforcement learning. However, long-term training often leads to issues like ineffective update accumulation (learning tax)...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, paper, arxiv, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Pareto-Optimal Offline Reinforcement Learning via Smooth Tchebysheff Scalarization
item.description: arXiv:2604.13175v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models can be aligned with human preferences through offline reinforcement learning (RL) on small labeled datasets. While single-objective alignment is well-studied, many real-world applications demand the simultaneous optimization of m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, alignment, paper, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Multi-Model Approach to English-Bangla Sentiment Classification of Government Mobile Banking App Reviews
item.description: arXiv:2604.13057v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: For millions of users in developing economies who depend on mobile banking as their primary gateway to financial services, app quality directly shapes financial access. The study analyzed 5,652 Google Play reviews in English and Bangla (filtered from ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, release, study, transformer, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Self-Monitoring Benefits from Structural Integration: Lessons from Metacognition in Continuous-Time Multi-Timescale Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.11914v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-monitoring capabilities -- metacognition, self-prediction, and subjective duration -- are often proposed as useful additions to reinforcement learning agents. But do they actually help? We investigate this question in a continuous-time multi-time...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, model, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sparse Goodness: How Selective Measurement Transforms Forward-Forward Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.13081v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Forward-Forward (FF) algorithm is a biologically plausible alternative to backpropagation that trains neural networks layer by layer using a local goodness function to distinguish positive from negative data. Since its introduction, sum-of-squares...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, analysis, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Long Delay to Arithmetic Generalization: When Learned Representations Outrun Behavior
item.description: arXiv:2604.13082v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Grokking in transformers trained on algorithmic tasks is characterized by a long delay between training-set fit and abrupt generalization, but the source of that delay remains poorly understood. In encoder-decoder arithmetic models, we argue that this...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, model, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Generalization Guarantees on Data-Driven Tuning of Gradient Descent with Langevin Updates
item.description: arXiv:2604.13130v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study learning to learn for regression problems through the lens of hyperparameter tuning. We propose the Langevin Gradient Descent Algorithm (LGD), which approximates the mean of the posterior distribution defined by the loss function and regulari...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, few-shot, few-shot learning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Narrative-Driven Paper-to-Slide Generation via ArcDeck
item.description: arXiv:2604.11969v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce ArcDeck, a multi-agent framework that formulates paper-to-slide generation as a structured narrative reconstruction task. Unlike existing methods that directly summarize raw text into slides, ArcDeck explicitly models the source paper's l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, paper, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Spectral Entropy Collapse as an Empirical Signature of Delayed Generalisation in Grokking
item.description: arXiv:2604.13123v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Grokking -- delayed generalisation long after memorisation -- lacks a predictive mechanistic explanation. We identify the normalised spectral entropy $\tilde{H}(t)$ of the representation covariance as a scalar order parameter for this transition, vali...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Synthetic Tabular Generators Fail to Preserve Behavioral Fraud Patterns: A Benchmark on Temporal, Velocity, and Multi-Account Signals
item.description: arXiv:2604.13125v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce behavioral fidelity -- a third evaluation dimension for synthetic tabular data that measures whether generated data preserves the temporal, sequential, and structural behavioral patterns that distinguish real-world entity activity. Existi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, release, analysis, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Depth-Resolved Coral Reef Thermal Fields from Satellite SST and Sparse In-Situ Loggers Using Physics-Informed Neural Networks
item.description: arXiv:2604.13131v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Satellite sea surface temperature (SST) products underpin global coral bleaching monitoring, yet they measure only the ocean skin. Corals inhabit depths from the shallows to beyond 20 metres, where temperatures can be 1-3{\deg}C cooler than the surfac...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, product | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
